description: Rules and substrates referenced by the Type-4 adversarial coverage matrix.

rules.summary: Represent one-level flat-map as a first-class HoF and value-graph node.
boundaries: Do not model depth-parameterized Array.prototype.flat(depth) as this rule.
Nested-list comprehensions remain Map-of-Map, not FlatMap.

rules.summary: Interpreter executes FlatMap and propagates lambda errors or non-list lambda results as Err.
boundaries: Oracle support must land before broadening HoF canonicalization.

rules.summary: Canonicalize option-producing filter-map forms to a shared representation.
boundaries: Must distinguish dropped None values from mapped Some values.
Falsey emitted values such as 0 must not be treated as absence.
Do not assume arbitrary callbacks are pure.

rules.summary: Teach aggregate consumers how to consume FlatMap streams without confusing them with filtered Map nodes.
boundaries: Map and FlatMap argument layouts are different; aggregate code must match the HoF kind explicitly.

rules.summary: Lower proven pure Java stream adapters into shared HoF/value-graph representations, including flatMap/map chains.
boundaries: Only the modeled pure stream subset is covered.
Stream.map returning streams remains nested and must not collapse to FlatMap.
Effectful callbacks and parallel stream semantics remain outside the pure idiom rule.

rules.summary: Interpreter support for the pure Java stream HoF subset, including expression-body lambda callbacks.
boundaries: Expression-body lambdas are evaluated as expressions; statement/block lambdas still execute through the statement path.
Effects inside callbacks are recorded by the oracle and are not proof of a pure canonicalization.
No parallel stream semantics.

rules.summary: Interpreter executes FilterMap-style HoF nodes: Err propagates, Null drops an item, and every other value is emitted.
boundaries: Null is the current absence sentinel; wrapped Some(None)-style payloads remain outside the modeled subset.
Must preserve Err/Null/value distinctions before any engine filter_map canon is accepted.

rules.summary: Canonicalize proof-backed integer min/max clamp compositions to a shared Clamp value.
boundaries: Requires a unique x/lo/hi candidate, integer-domain facts, and a lo<=hi proof.
Missing or non-exiting guards, swapped bounds, wrong nesting, and float/NaN-sensitive domains stay fail-closed.
Two-comparison ternary and library clamp forms are tracked separately by numeric.clamp_surface_bridge.

rules.summary: Bridge two-comparison and language library clamp forms to the shared Clamp value.
boundaries: Must reuse the same integer-domain and lo<=hi proof as numeric.clamp_composition.
Do not treat method names or bound parameter names as proof.

rules.summary: Recover conservative facts that prove lower bound order for clamp-like canons from literal bounds and exiting inverse guards.
boundaries: No order fact means no clamp canon.
Non-exiting checks and name-only lower/upper conventions are not proof.

rules.summary: Prove receiver/key/default identity for collection and map-default idioms across local/module/import boundaries.
boundaries: Mutation, shadowing, and unresolved imports stay fail-closed.
Requires proven receiver/key/default coordinates.

rules.summary: Keep HoF value-graph growth and semantic scan runtime bounded while coverage expands.
boundaries: Coverage wins that expand fingerprints without bound are perf-blocked until represented compactly.
